CHARTS / BASKETS
% RETURN, REBASED · EOD 2026-06-04
Rotation ReadCitrini · claude

Tech is eating everything else and it isn't subtle. XLK ripped +15.5% in a month while every other sector posted single-digit moves or losses, and the YTD spread between XLK at +32.6% and XLF at -5.4% tells you this isn't a rising tide — it's a vertical funnel into one trade. Underneath the index, AI infra ran +38% in a month with DELL leading, semis +30.3% behind MRVL, cyber +33% behind FTNT. You are watching the entire risk budget of the market get reallocated into the silicon-and-cables stack in real time.

The tension: this is a melt-up with no participation. XLU got hit for -5.8% on the month, XLY is negative YTD, XLC is negative YTD, and Mag 7 averaged only +3.4% — meaning the AAPL/AMZN cohort is being LEFT BEHIND by the second-derivative AI names. That's not 'broadening' — that's narrowing inside the narrow trade. Energy YTD +28.5% is the only non-tech story still standing, and even it cooled -0.9% this month.

The stretched ratios are screaming. XLK/XLU at z=3.75 and 99th percentile, MTUM/SPY at z=3.47, SPHB/SPLV at z=2.74 — every single 'risk-on vs defensive' pair is pinned at the historical ceiling. Meanwhile XLC, XLY, XLU and MCHI sit at the 1st-2nd percentile vs SPY. When the spread between cyclicals and defensives is THIS dislocated, you don't fight it from the long side anymore — you start sizing the unwind.

Vertical AI melt-up vs. zero participation outside the silicon stack

BULL

AI infra +38%/mo, semis +30%/mo, cyber +33%/mo — the capex cycle is real, FTNT/DELL/MRVL are pricing acceleration, and SPHB/SPLV at z=2.74 says high-beta still wants to run.

BEAR

XLU -5.8%, XLY negative YTD, Mag 7 only +3.4%/mo, XLC/XLY/MCHI all at 1st-2nd percentile vs SPY — the market is funneling into one trade while everything else rolls, which historically ends with the leader catching down, not the laggards catching up.

TELL

XLK/XLU at z=3.75, 99th pctile. The first weekly close that breaks this ratio's trend is the rotation signal — until then, fade nothing in tech.

⚠ Sector rotation reads as full risk-on: XLK +15.5%/mo, XLF positive on the month, SPHB/SPLV at z=2.74 vs But XLY is **-0.7% YTD** and XLC is **-2.4% YTD** — the consumer and comms are NOT participating in the supposed risk-on — True risk-on broadens; this rally is funneling into AI infrastructure exclusively. Trust the breadth read (b) — the rotation is narrow and fragile, not broad.
⚠ MTUM/SPY at z=3.47 says momentum factor is dominant vs VLUE/SPY also at z=2.88, both 99th percentile simultaneously — Momentum AND value extreme together is unusual — typically they trade off. Likely both are loaded with the same AI-adjacent names, meaning factor diversification is broken. Trust neither as an independent signal.
⚠ Energy XLE -1.4%/mo, basket -0.9%/mo suggests crude/cyclical weakness vs Energy YTD still +28.5%, second only to XLK — A short-term cooldown in a still-leading sector is consolidation, not rotation out. Trust the YTD trend (b) until XLE breaks its uptrend.

Eight ratios sit at |z|>=2.5 and they cluster into one trade: long high-beta tech, short defensives/intl. XLK/XLU at z=3.75 (99th), XLK/SPY at z=3.72 (99th), MTUM/SPY at z=3.47, SOXX/SMH at z=3.46 and 100th percentile — these are confirmed trend, not reversion candidates yet, but they're the kind of readings that mark exhaustion when they finally crack. The cleaner mean-reversion setups are the mirror images: XLC/SPY at z=-3.36, XLY/SPY at z=-2.73, MCHI/SPY at z=-2.64 — these are the 'left for dead' trades that historically snap back hard when leadership rotates. EWT/SPY at z=3.04 (Taiwan, semis proxy) and QQQ/TLT at z=2.91 confirm the AI-funded duration short. You want to be early on the unwind, not first.
